message:
fix(cli): doctor repair preservation, tooling hardening, preview fixes

A bundle of CLI-side fixes that all share the same philosophy as the
recent db/reconstruct changes: prefer preserving rows and surfacing
errors to silently deleting data or swallowing failures.

== doctor repair: preserve rather than delete ==

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs

- Introduce DoctorRecipientCleanupSummary { deleted_rows,
  preserved_missing_agent_rows, affected_agents } and change
  doctor_cleanup_orphaned_message_recipients() to return it instead
  of a bare (usize, usize).  The old signature conflated "deleted"
  and "affected" counts and had no way to surface rows that were
  intentionally preserved.

- Split orphaned recipients into two buckets:
    - dangling_message_rows: the parent message no longer exists
      (genuinely dangling; safe to delete).
    - missing_agent only: the message is fine, only the agent row
      is gone — these are now left in place so the reconstruct
      path (see sibling commit) can still render them as
      `[unknown-agent-N]`.
  Only dangling_message_rows are fed to the DELETE loop and to
  doctor_rebuild_inbox_stats_for_agents.

- Guard inbox-stats rebuild with `if !affected_agents.is_empty()`
  so the helper isn't called with an empty slice in the now-common
  "everything was missing-agent, nothing to delete" case.

- handle_doctor_repair_with():
    - Orphan messages (message whose project_id is missing) are
      no longer deleted.  They're reported as
      "Orphan messages: N (preserved; project metadata row
      missing)" and left in place so the missing project row can be
      restored later.
    - Switch to the new summary struct:
        "Dangling message recipients: N ..."   (was "Orphaned...")
        "Cleaned dangling message recipients: N ..."
        "Preserved recipient rows with missing agent metadata: N"
    - Update the confirm_mutating_doctor_action prompt from
      "delete orphaned rows" → "clean dangling recipient rows" to
      reflect the narrower scope.

- New regression tests:
    - doctor_repair_preserves_message_recipients_when_only_agent_metadata_is_missing
    - doctor_repair_preserves_messages_when_project_metadata_is_missing
  Both build a disk-backed DB, insert the pathological row(s),
  run handle_doctor_repair_with(..., dry_run=false, yes=true),
  and assert the rows survive and that the expected
  "preserved" line appears in stdout.

== tooling locks: surface storage errors, include archive metadata ==

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs

- New ToolingLockReport struct { archive_root, exists, raw_locks }
  with a .payload() helper that serializes it to the JSON shape the
  viewer expects (archive_root + exists + total + locks).
- New tooling_lock_report_from_result() — factored for unit testing —
  that maps a storage Result into either CliError::Other (carrying
  the storage root path and the underlying error) or a populated
  report.
- New tooling_lock_report() thin wrapper over
  mcp_agent_mail_storage::collect_lock_status().
- handle_tooling_locks() rewritten to use the new helpers instead of
  the previous `unwrap_or_else(|_e| json!({...empty...}))` which
  silently hid storage errors.  The table output now also prints
  Archive root / Exists, and prints "Archive root does not exist."
  warn() when exists=false so users can tell the difference between
  "empty archive" and "archive not initialized".
- New tests:
    - tooling_lock_report_includes_archive_metadata
    - tooling_lock_report_surfaces_storage_errors
      (verifies CliError::Other message contains both the path and
       the storage error detail)

== sql rendering: preserve array values with non-finite numbers ==

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs

- tooling_sql_value_to_text() used serde_json::to_string(&items)
  directly on Value::Array(items).  If any element was a Double
  containing NaN/Inf, serialization would fail and the whole array
  rendered as an empty string, silently losing every other element.
- Route each element through tooling_sql_value_to_json() and then
  serialize the resulting Value::Array.  That helper already maps
  non-finite doubles to JSON null, so we lose one bad cell instead
  of the entire row.
- New test tooling_sql_value_to_text_preserves_array_values_with_non_finite_numbers
  asserts `[NaN, "ok", 7]` → `[null, "ok", 7]`.

== preview server: exact path match + accurate file count ==

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs

- start_preview_server(): change the /__preview__/status dispatch
  from `path.starts_with("/__preview__/status")` to `path ==
  "/__preview__/status"`.  The starts_with match meant
  `/__preview__/status-extra` (or any path prefixed by that) would
  be treated as the status endpoint instead of a 404.
- collect_preview_status(): count files using file_entries.len()
  rather than parts.len().  parts included the manual signature
  token, so files_indexed was always N+1.
- New tests:
    - http_get "/__preview__/status-extra" now asserts 404
      "Not Found" inside the existing preview server harness.
    - collect_preview_status_counts_only_real_files builds a tiny
      bundle dir and asserts files_indexed == 2.

== cli output: surface serialization errors in json mode ==

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/output.rs

- json_or_table() in json_mode was calling
  `serde_json::to_string_pretty(data).unwrap_or_else(|_| "[]")`
  which silently printed "[]" on any Serialize failure.  Replace
  with encode_json_pretty_or_error() (already used elsewhere in
  this file) so the caller sees
  `{"error":"output serialization failed: ..."}` instead of a
  misleading empty array.
- New test json_or_table_json_mode_surfaces_serialization_errors
  uses a failing-Serialize shim to prove the new error JSON comes
  out and the table renderer closure is *not* called.

== Cargo.lock ==

- Transitive refresh: fsqlite-wal (or its sibling workspace crate)
  grew an edge on dashmap.  No direct dependency changes on our
  side.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
